concerns.text: Task T019 implements 'fractal dimension calculation' (box-counting method). This metric is NOT present in spec.md FR-003 (which lists only branch points, total length, soma area, Sholl intersections) nor in the plan.md. This is unapproved scope (gold-plating) and creates a task with no corresponding spec requirement.

Task T019 violates plan.md Constitution Check VI ('Neuro-Image Quantification Rigor'), which mandates metrics derived from 'specific plugins (Simple Neurite Tracer or Microglial Morphometry)'. The 'box-counting method' for fractal dimension is not part of the mandated plugin set, creating a task that contradicts the plan's constitutional constraints.

Tasks T006a and T006b reference the path 'specs/001-investigating-the-role-of-microglial-mor/contracts/'. However, plan.md 'Project Structure' defines the path as 'specs/001-gene-regulation/contracts/'. This path mismatch means the tasks will fail to create files in the location defined by the plan, breaking dependencies (T007, T018).

Tasks T012a, T012b, and T018 rely on 'synthetic data' for validation. However, spec.md FR-001 and FR-008 explicitly require the system to 'ingest high-resolution confocal microscopy images from the Allen Brain Atlas and AD Knowledge Portal' and 'validate data availability... before ingestion'. There is no task to implement the actual fetching, checksumming, or ingestion logic for these real external datasets, leaving the core data ingestion requirement unimplemented.

Task T035 calculates 'absolute percentage deviation' of p-values. Spec.md SC-003 requires verifying 'variation in the primary interaction effect p-value' to ensure stability. Percentage deviation of a p-value (which can be near zero) is mathematically unstable and not the standard metric for stability (which should be absolute difference or log-transformed ratio). The task implements a metric that does not satisfy the success criterion defined in SC-003.
